markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto VI multiple times, shifting the official release from a 2025 window to May 26, 2026, and most recently November 19, 2026, extending the period for other cultural or entertainment milestones to occur first. This extended timeline fuels trader focus on high-profile releases in music, technology, and pop culture that could resolve before the game's launch. Rockstar's reputation for polish and massive marketing campaigns typically builds significant pre-release buzz, yet the repeated postponements highlight development complexities common in blockbuster titles. Key upcoming catalysts include potential new trailers, seasonal promotional events, and concurrent album drops or platform updates that could sway momentum in a market sensitive to verified announcements and release schedules.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s New York rape retrial—declared May 15 after a deadlocked jury—has solidified trader consensus around no additional prison time at 79.6% implied probability. With the 74-year-old already serving a 16-year California sentence and facing documented health complications that have kept him in and out of Rikers medical care, the unresolved charge leaves little immediate prospect of further incarceration from this case. Precursor proceedings, including two prior mistrials and an overturned 2020 conviction, underscore the persistent evidentiary and procedural hurdles. Upcoming developments remain limited, as prosecutors have not signaled plans to retry the matter imminently, keeping market focus on the low likelihood of near-term sentencing outcomes in the 5–30-year ranges.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights lead the 2026 Stanley Cup champion market because they are the two teams competing in the ongoing Stanley Cup Final. The Hurricanes posted the Eastern Conference's top regular-season mark at 53-22-7 for 113 points and advanced through the playoffs with dominant series wins. The Golden Knights, finishing at 39-26-17, built momentum with a six-game win streak and a Western Conference Final sweep of the Presidents' Trophy-winning Avalanche. Current series results and home-ice dynamics have produced modest shifts in implied probabilities, keeping both outcomes closely contested as the best-of-seven matchup continues.

New York’s 53-29 regular-season mark, strong defensive metrics, and experience navigating the Eastern Conference bracket have sustained momentum despite the narrow loss. San Antonio’s resurgence behind Victor Wembanyama’s dominant scoring and rebounding in the series has tightened the gap, yet the Spurs trail and face the challenge of evening the matchup on the road. Recent form, home-court dynamics in New York, and both teams’ health entering the later stages of the playoffs continue to shape the probabilities reflected in current pricing.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: MegaETH airdrop by...?
events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France share the narrowest edge among 2026 World Cup contenders at 16.1% implied probability each, reflecting trader consensus on their elite squad depth, recent European Championship pedigree, and favorable FIFA rankings near the top. England follows at 10.5% after a flawless qualifying campaign, while Portugal and Argentina sit just behind amid strong attacking options and historical pedigree. The tight clustering among the top six teams stems from comparable recent form, balanced group-stage paths, and limited standout injuries heading into the expanded 48-team field opening June 11, leaving outcomes highly sensitive to early tournament results and fitness developments.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and the absence of a designated successor, supporting trader expectations that he will remain paramount leader past 2027. Term limits on the presidency were eliminated in 2018, enabling a precedent-breaking third term in 2022, and analyses indicate he is positioned for a fourth term at the 2027 Party Congress. Recent diplomatic activity, including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and a planned June visit to North Korea, underscores his active role without signs of internal challenges or health-related constraints. Potential successors remain unpromoted to the Politburo Standing Committee, reinforcing continuity over transition.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the highest trader-implied probability in the fragmented 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, reflecting his position as California governor with executive governing experience and repeated national media exposure. The wide field remains open more than two years before primaries, with no candidates having formally launched campaigns as of mid-2026. Contenders differentiate along lines of ideological positioning, Senate or House records, state-level executive tenure, and appeal to distinct voter coalitions. Recent polls have shown variable leads between Newsom and Kamala Harris, while figures such as Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draw support from progressive voters. The 2026 midterm elections and any subsequent shifts in party leadership or polling trends could accelerate consolidation around one or more frontrunners before formal primary voting begins.
